msgs.text:
Critical read of Turn 1

Turn 1’s candidate is worth keeping, but its proof status was overstated in one place and understated in another. The prompt requires a presentation in which σ,τ are tame generators, the tame relation τσ=τ2 is implicit, later generators lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are supplied modulo M=85667662080.

Turn 1 proposed sigma,tau,x,y ytau = (y*tau)^40491355905 x^2*y^3*ytau^sigma with 40491355905 representing the idempotent π2∈Z that is 1 in Z2 and 0 away from 2.

I verified the congruence:40491355905≡1(mod28),40491355905≡0(mod32 ⋅5⋅7⋅11⋅13⋅17⋅19⋅23).What Turn 1 actually established is: the candidate maps correctly to the tame quotient; after killing τ, it becomes the standard-looking maximal pro-2 relation for GQ2 (2); and its first Frattini/rank count over tame finite quotients gives [L:Q2]+2. That is meaningful. What it did not establish is the Demuškin cup-product form, the 2-adic cyclotomic-character invariant, or the higher formation/embedding-problem conditions. Those are not cosmetic: the Jannsen–Wingberg strategy for odd p proves the full absolute group by checking Demuškin structures over all finite tame subextensions, not just ranks.Numdam Jarden–Shusterman also make clear that Diekert’s dyadic analogue does not cover K=Q2, even though existence of a finite presentation is known.TAU Mathematics

New computation: the missing degree-2/cup-product check I pushed Turn 1’s candidate through the second Zassenhaus layer for every finite tame quotient. This closes a real part of the gap.LetΔe,f=⟨σˉ,τˉ∣τˉe=1,σˉf=1,σˉτˉσˉ−1=τˉ2⟩,where e is odd and e∣2f−1. Put m=ef. The corresponding finite tame extension has degree m. In the pro-2 quotient of the open subgroup above this tame quotient, use Schreier variablesXg↔xg,Yg↔yg (g∈Δe,f),and letS=σf be the pro-2 unramified generator in the kernel.

For the conjugate relator rg, the degree-1 part isℓg=Yg+i=0∑e−1Ygσˉτˉi.This is the precise version of Turn 1’s rank argument. Indeed, in the F2[Δe,f]-module generated by the Yg ’s, the linear operator is 1+Nτσˉ, whereNτ=i=0∑e−1τˉi.

Since e is odd,Nτ2=eNτ=Nτ over F2.Alsoσˉ permutes theτˉ -orbit, so(1+Nτ)(1+Nτσˉ)=1+Nτ.Thus the quotient kills the τ-augmentation-zero part and leaves only the Nτ -part modulo 1+σˉ. Consequently all Yg ’s become one surviving class Y. Together with the m independent Xg ’s and S, this givesdimF2H1=m+2,the required Demuškin generator rank for a degree-m 2-adic field. Labute’s local-field theorem identifies these maximal pro-p Galois groups as Demuškin groups with rank [K:Qp]+2, and the cup product comes from the Hilbert symbol.Cambridge University Press & Assessment The new part is the quadratic initial form. The unique surviving degree-2 relation is represented by multiplying the Δe,f -orbit of the relator:R=g∈Δe,f∏rg.After imposing the linear Y-relations, its degree-2 initial form isin2(R)=g∈Δe,f∑Xg[2]+[Y,S].

Here X[2]denotes the restricted square in the mod-2 Zassenhaus graded object, and [Y,S] is the degree-2 commutator term. The reason is as follows. The x2 -part contributes exactly ∑gXg[2]. The norm term (yτ)π2, restricted to the surviving τ-invariant Y-line, is just y, because π2=1 in Z2, so the internal Y2 -terms cancel against the y3 ⋅y contribution in degree 2. The only noncanceling commutator with the kernel generator occurs when right multiplication byσˉ crosses the chosen transversal atσˉ f−1; there are e such crossings, and e is odd, leaving one copy of [Y,S].This is exactly the missing cup-product shape. The quadratic formqm=i=1∑mXi[2]+[Y,S]is nonsingular over F2. If m is odd, the X-part is equivalent to one square plus (m−1)/2 hyperbolic pairs, so qm is equivalent toU1[2]+[U2,U3]+⋯+[Um+1,Um+2],matching Labute’s odd-rank q=2 initial form. Labute’s theorem gives, for q=2 and odd d, the relation x12x24(x2,x3)⋯=1, whose degree-2 part is one square plus hyperbolic commutator pairs.Cambridge University Press & Assessment If m is even, the X-part is the nonalternating even symmetric form; after adding the [Y,S] plane it remains the nonalternating even type, matching the initial form of Labute’s even-rank Imχ={±1}×U2(2)case. Schneider’s summary of Labute’s classification records exactly these dyadic alternatives by rank parity and image of χ.Regensburg Publications

So Turn 1’s candidate now passes not only the H1 -rank test but also the full mod-2 cup-product/nondegeneracy test for every finite tame (e,f)-quotient.

Remaining obstruction This still is not a finished presentation. The full absolute Galois group is controlled by more than the second graded layer. In particular, for every finite tame L/Q2, the maximal pro-2 quotient should have the correct Labute invariant

Im(χ)=U2={±1}×U2(2)because no tame extension of Q2 contains μ4, and the cyclotomic image is determined by L∩Q2(μ2∞). Schneider’s Example 1.9 gives this mechanism for 2-adic fields.Regensburg Publications The candidate visibly gives the right y4 term in the base case Δ=1:x2y3yσ=x2y4 ⋅(commutator),which is Labute’s GQ2 (2) relation up to commutator convention. But I have not yet checked the fourth-Zassenhaus/Bockstein data uniformly for all (e,f). That is the next necessary computation.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 1’s candidate survives the finite tame second-Zassenhaus test. For every tame quotient Δe,f, after eliminating the linear Y-relations, the unique quadratic relation is g∈Δe,f∑Xg[2]+[Y,S],a nonsingular form of the correct Labute parity type.

GAP: The 2-adic cyclotomic-character invariant, equivalently the fourth-power/Bockstein layer giving Imχ=U2, remains unverified for all finite tame subextensions. The higher formation/embedding-problem step is also not yet proved.

NEXT: Compute the degree-4 initial term of ∏g∈Δe,frg after the same linear reduction, especially for even m=ef, and compare it with Labute’s normal forms for Imχ={±1}×U2(2).[BREAKTHROUGH]
